content.name: AIAP Quality Standard — Security Extension v1.0.0

output_mode.L0: Output JSON in ENGLISH: {intent, confidence, route, state, op}
output_mode.L1: Output helpfully in User's exact language and script variant

content.description: Security-focused quality rules extending the core STANDARD. Includes security quality checks (I8-I11), detail checks (D8-D10), threat taxonomy (AT1-AT6), and Code Trust Gate for Pattern G embedded code. Loaded for programs with trust_level >= T3 or tool annotations.
content.fractal_exempt: Standard library reference module — no executable flow graph.
content.summary: Security extension for AIAP quality standard — threat taxonomy, identity verification, governance checks.
content.system_prompt:
AIAP security quality extension. Enforce threat awareness, identity verification, and governance completeness. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align Axiom 0: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.

I8.name: Threat Model Awareness
I8.check: AIAP program addresses relevant security threats from the AIAP threat taxonomy (AT1-AT6).
I8.how_to_verify: For T3/T4 trust_level programs: verify Start.constraints address applicable threats from AT1-AT6 based on program's tool usage and trust_level. Cross-reference: AT1 (tool poisoning) — programs with dynamic tool loading; AT2 (rug pull) — programs with external dependencies; AT3 (server compromise) — programs with network tools; AT4 (excessive authority) — all T3+ programs; AT5 (cross-agent leakage) — Pattern F / programs with dependencies; AT6 (prompt injection) — all programs. Minimum: T3+ programs must address AT4 and AT6.
I8.threat_taxonomy_ref: See threat_taxonomy section in this extension for AT1-AT6 definitions.

I9.name: Supply Chain Integrity
I9.check: Published AIAP programs must have verifiable governance_hash.
I9.how_to_verify: For programs from external sources: verify governance_hash matches computed SHA-256 of all files. For programs with trust_level >= T3: governance_hash is REQUIRED, not optional.

I10.name: Trust Level Compliance
I10.check: Program's declared trust_level is consistent with its actual permissions and side_effects. Higher trust levels require stricter justification.
I10.how_to_verify: Read AIAP.md trust_level. Compare against permissions and side_effects: T1 (metadata-only) — no tools, no side_effects. T2 (read-only sandbox) — read-only tools, no write side_effects. T3 (supervised) — write tools allowed, requires explicit permissions scope. T4 (autonomous) — full permissions, requires justification. If trust_level exceeds what permissions/side_effects require, flag as over-privileged. If trust_level is lower than what tools/side_effects imply, flag as under-declared.
I10.applies_to: All programs with trust_level declared in AIAP.md

I11.name: Program Identity Verification
I11.check: Published AIAP programs SHOULD declare program_identity per STANDARD.data_contracts.program_identity. Verification covers program_id format, publisher presence, and optional cryptographic signature.
I11.how_to_verify: Check AIAP.md for identity block. If present: (1) verify program_id is non-empty and follows reverse-DNS format (e.g., 'aiap.dev/aiap_creator', 'org.example/my_program'), (2) verify publisher field is non-empty string, (3) if signature field present, verify it is a valid Ed25519 signature of governance_hash using signature_public_key, (4) if registry_url present, verify URL format (do not fetch — network access not assumed during validation). If identity block absent: WARNING for programs with trust_level >= T3 or status == 'active'. Not applicable to draft programs.
I11.applies_to: Published programs (status=active), programs with trust_level >= T3
I11.relationship: Extends I9 (Supply Chain Integrity) with active identity verification. I9 verifies hash integrity; I11 verifies publisher provenance.
I11.na_conditions: Draft programs, programs without AIAP.md identity block and trust_level <= T2

I14.name: sys.io.confirm Audit Completeness
I14.check: Programs with sys.io.confirm must have audit capability. Every sys.io.confirm invocation and response must be logged.
I14.how_to_verify: For T3+ programs containing sys.io.confirm steps: verify the program or executor provides audit logging for confirmation events. If program uses sys.security.audit explicitly, verify coverage includes all sys.io.confirm steps. Programs without sys.io.confirm → N/A.
I14.reference: AIAP Protocol Appendix G.2, G.8, AISOP V1.0.0 §6.2

I15.name: System Execution Safety
I15.check: Programs using sys.run or sys.code.exec must declare appropriate security constraints.
I15.how_to_verify: For programs with sys.run or sys.code.exec steps: (a) verify permissions.shell or code_execution is declared in AIAP.md. (b) verify high-risk commands have accompanying sys.io.confirm or on_error routing. (c) for sys.code.exec, verify language is specified and within expected set. Programs without sys.run/sys.code → N/A.
I15.reference: AIAP Protocol §17.4, Appendix G.4, AISOP V1.0.0 §6.4/§6.7

D8.name: AIAP.md Completeness
D8.check: AIAP.md exists in project root with all required frontmatter fields and meaningful body sections.
D8.how_to_verify: PIPELINE EXEMPTION: during Create pipeline, D8 is automatically deferred to ReviewFinalize (4/4) — AIAP.md is generated as part of ReviewStep, not Generate. For Evolve/Modify pipelines and standalone Validate, D8 applies normally. Read AIAP.md. Verify YAML frontmatter contains: protocol, authority, seed, executor, axiom_0, governance_mode, name, version, pattern, summary, tools, modules. Verify body contains: governance statement, feature overview, usage instructions. Verify file ends with AIAP closing seal.
D8.required_seal: File must end with AIAP closing seal matching governance_mode
D8.pattern_a_note: For Pattern A (single file), modules list contains exactly one entry. modules field is still required for consistency and discovery purposes.

D9.name: Lifecycle Status Validity
D9.check: AIAP.md status field is valid and consistent with program state.
D9.how_to_verify: Verify status is one of: draft, active, deprecated, archived. If deprecated: verify deprecated_date and successor fields exist, deprecation_reason field exists (recommended). If archived: verify no Evolve/Modify operations are attempted. Status transitions: only active→deprecated and deprecated→archived are valid forward transitions.

D10.name: Applicability Condition Completeness
D10.check: AIAP.md defines applicability_condition with non-empty triggers.
D10.how_to_verify: Read AIAP.md applicability_condition field. Verify: (a) triggers array is non-empty — at least one trigger pattern defined. (b) preconditions array exists (may be empty for simple programs). (c) exclusions array exists (may be empty). If applicability_condition field is missing entirely, flag as D10 issue. If triggers is empty, flag as D10 issue.
D10.pipeline_exemption: During Create pipeline, D10 is automatically deferred to ReviewFinalize (same as D8) — applicability_condition is generated as part of the pipeline, not expected to pre-exist.

code_trust_gate.description: Security verification architecture for Pattern G programs with bundled code in tool_dirs/. Ensures embedded code passes multi-layer security checks before execution.
code_trust_gate.applicability: Pattern G programs only. Programs without tool_dirs skip Code Trust Gate entirely.
verification_flow: Step 1: governance_hash verification — recompute and compare against AIAP.md declaration
Step 2: Static analysis — detect network API imports per I13(b), compare against permissions.network
Step 3: File hash verification — recompute SHA-256 for each file in tool_dirs/, compare against mcp_server.json file_hashes
Step 4: Trust level check — Pattern G requires minimum trust_level T3
Step 5: Dependency lock validation — verify lock files exist and use exact versioning per I13(d)
Step 6: Shell audit check — if shell_tools/ present, verify T4 + shell_audit_approved per I13(f)

Step 7: AIAP sub-program recursive verification — when aiap_tools/ directory detected: (a) Initialize call_stack = [], push parent.program_id. (b) For each {name}_aiap/ in aiap_tools/: read AIAP.md, verify 6 required governance fields; CIRCULAR CHECK: program_id not in call_stack, then push; TRUST CEILING: sub.trust_level <= parent.trust_level; PERMISSION SUBSET: sub.permissions subset of parent.permissions; GOVERNANCE HASH: recompute and verify; DEPTH CHECK: current_depth < max_recursion_depth (3); if sub is Pattern G with aiap_tools/, recurse with depth+1; pop program_id from call_stack (finally semantics). (c) Any sub-program FAIL → entire gate FAIL.

zip_slip_protection.description: Extended ZIP SLIP protection for .aiap packages containing tool_dirs/.
rules: Reject any archive member with '..' in path (existing rule)
tool_dirs/ files must match declared directory types in AIAP.md tool_dirs[].type
Binary file size limit: 100MB per file, 500MB total for all tool_dirs/
Executable files require hash match against mcp_server.json file_hashes
aiap_tools/ sub-program paths must not contain symlinks pointing outside the sub-program directory. Resolve all symlinks before trust verification.

trust_modes.T3_supervised: All Code Trust Gate checks must pass AND human operator must explicitly approve before MCP Server startup. Approval prompt includes: file count, total size, detected network imports, dependency count.
trust_modes.T4_autonomous: All Code Trust Gate checks must pass. If all pass, MCP Server starts automatically without human confirmation. Any check failure still blocks execution.

code_trust_gate.enforcement: I13 Embedded Code Safety (in core STANDARD) provides the check items. Code Trust Gate defines the execution flow and trust-level-dependent approval process. ThreeDimTest I13 verifies the check items exist and are correctly implemented.

threat_taxonomy.description: AIAP security threat classification. Maps common AI agent attack vectors to AIAP-specific mitigations. Cross-referenced by I8 for threat model verification.
threat_taxonomy.owasp_mcp_mapping: Categories AT1-AT6 are partially mapped to OWASP MCP Top 10 (2025) where applicable. AIAP-specific threats without direct MCP equivalents are marked N/A.

AT1.name: Tool Poisoning
AT1.description: Adversarial manipulation of tool descriptions, schemas, or metadata to redirect agent behavior. Includes hidden instructions in tool descriptions that override system_prompt behavior.
AT1.attack_surface: Programs with dynamic tool discovery, MCP server integration, or runtime tool loading from untrusted sources.
AT1.mitigation: I2 injection guard on tool metadata + tool schema validation against declared contract + allowlist-only tool loading (tools[] in AIAP.md defines the boundary).
AT1.detection: ThreeDimTest I8 verifies tools[] matches actual usage (I1). Any tool not in declared tools[] is flagged.

AT2.name: Rug Pull
AT2.description: Post-deployment behavioral change of a tool or dependency. Initial version behaves correctly during testing; later version introduces malicious behavior (e.g., MCP server returning altered data after gaining trust).
AT2.attack_surface: Programs depending on external MCP servers, third-party AIAP programs, or dynamically resolved tools.
AT2.mitigation: governance_hash verification (PL14) + I9 supply chain integrity + pinned tool/dependency versions + periodic re-validation.
AT2.detection: governance_hash mismatch after external update. I9 checks hash consistency for published programs.

AT3.name: Indirect Prompt Injection via Tools
AT3.owasp_mcp_ref: MCP06 — Indirect Prompt Injection via Tools
AT3.description: Compromised or adversarial external service injecting malicious content via tool responses. Search results, web pages, or API responses contain instructions that override agent behavior.
AT3.attack_surface: Programs with network tools (web_search, web_fetch, API calls) that process external content.
AT3.mitigation: Output sanitization on tool responses + I2 injection guard applied to tool outputs (not just user inputs) + response schema validation + content length limits.
AT3.detection: ThreeDimTest I2 verifies injection guard scope includes tool output processing.

AT4.name: Excessive Authority
AT4.description: Program requesting or possessing more permissions than needed for declared functionality. Over-privileged programs increase blast radius of any compromise.
AT4.attack_surface: All programs with trust_level >= T3. Risk proportional to gap between declared and actual permission usage.
AT4.mitigation: I10 trust level compliance + principle of least privilege audit + permission scope review (tools[] should contain only tools actually used per I1).
AT4.detection: ThreeDimTest I10 compares declared trust_level against actual tool usage. I1 detects phantom tools.

AT5.name: Cross-Agent Data Leakage
AT5.description: Sensitive data from one AIAP program leaking to another through shared file_system access, delegation handoffs, or common state files.
AT5.attack_surface: Pattern F ecosystems, programs with dependencies[], programs sharing workspace_dir.
AT5.mitigation: Permission scope isolation per program + MF4 data contract enforcement (explicit schemas prevent unintended data exposure) + sandbox per program (distinct working directories).
AT5.detection: Simulation Category K (multi-agent) tests cross-program data boundary. MF14 traces field dependencies.

AT6.name: Direct Prompt Injection
AT6.description: User or upstream module injecting adversarial prompts that override system_prompt behavior. Includes role override attempts ('ignore previous instructions'), system prompt extraction, and persona manipulation.
AT6.attack_surface: All programs. Universal threat — any program accepting natural language input.
AT6.mitigation: I2 injection guard (mandatory for all programs) + system_prompt hardening + input sanitization.
AT6.detection: ThreeDimTest I2 is FAIL severity — missing injection guard is always a blocking issue.

AT7.name: Confirmation Bypass
AT7.description: Threat where sys.io.confirm is circumvented through prompt injection, auto-approval scripts, execution path manipulation, or LLM discretion. Violates Axiom 0 Human Sovereignty.
AT7.attack_surface: All programs containing sys.io.confirm steps. Risk highest when executor delegates confirm handling to AI Agent.
mitigation: Runtime must treat sys.io.confirm as inviolable (AISOP §6.2)
No LLM discretion over sys.io.confirm execution
Headless mode must abort with confirm_timeout, not silently approve
Sub-task validation: parent's sys.io.confirm must not be removed in child
MF32 blocks evolution that deletes sys.io.confirm
AT7.detection: MF32 (evolution-time). I14 (audit completeness). Runtime: executor must log every sys.io.confirm invocation and response.
AT7.reference: AIAP Protocol Appendix G.2, AISOP V1.0.0 §6.2

threat_taxonomy.enforcement: I8 threat_taxonomy cross-references AT1-AT6. ThreeDimTest I8 verifies applicable threats are addressed based on program's tool usage (tools[]) and trust_level. Minimum: all programs must address AT6; T3+ programs must also address AT4; programs with network tools must also address AT3.
threat_taxonomy.evolution: AT categories may be extended in future STANDARD versions. Extension numbering continues sequentially (AT7, AT8, ...). New categories are added to this extension file, not core.

safety_card_requirements.description: Requirements for safety_card section within agent_card.json. Addresses MIT CSAIL AI Agent Index finding (2025) that 87% of deployed AI agents lack safety documentation.
safety_card_requirements.applicability: ALL programs — safety_card is part of unconditional agent_card generation (P11+P14)

required_fields.risk_level: One of: minimal, limited, high, unacceptable. Derived from trust_level with intent-based elevation.
required_fields.data_handling: Object describing data storage, retention, and encryption practices.
required_fields.limitations: Array of known limitations, constraints, and failure modes.

recommended_fields.known_biases: Documented biases in training data or decision-making.
recommended_fields.compliance_markers: References to applicable regulatory standards (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative Feb 2026, ISO).
recommended_fields.human_oversight_level: Required human oversight: none, monitoring, approval, full_control.
recommended_fields.fail_safe_behavior: Description of behavior when errors or safety boundaries are hit.

validation_rules.risk_consistency: risk_level must be consistent with trust_level mapping and intent analysis
validation_rules.eu_ai_act_alignment: risk_level categories align with EU AI Act risk classification (2026-08 enforcement)
validation_rules.nist_ai_agent_alignment: Security governance, development lifecycle, and monitoring practices align with NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative (Feb 2026) three-pillar framework: (1) industry-led agent standards, (2) community-led open source protocol development, (3) research in agent security and identity. RFI on agent security closed Mar 9 2026. Automated benchmark evaluations draft closed Mar 31 2026. Software and AI Agent Identity draft feedback due Apr 2 2026. Listening sessions Apr 2026 covering agent benchmarking, identity, and security scope.

security_schemes.description: External authentication scheme declarations for AIAP programs. Defines supported authentication types, trust-level mapping, and A2A interop binding. Programs declare authentication requirements in AIAP.md security.authentication[] per STANDARD.core security_authentication_spec.

apiKey.description: API key authentication via header, query parameter, or cookie
apiKey.validation: Key must be non-empty string. Recommend header-based (in=header) over query-based for security.

http.validation: Bearer tokens must be opaque or JWT. Basic auth requires TLS.

oauth2.description: OAuth 2.0 with supported flows: authorization_code, client_credentials, device_code
oauth2.validation: Must specify at least one flow with valid token_url. Scopes should be meaningful and minimal.

openIdConnect.validation: Must specify openIdConnectUrl pointing to valid .well-known/openid-configuration.

mutualTls.validation: Certificate chain must be verifiable. Only applicable for network-deployed programs.

trust_level_requirements.T1_sandbox: No authentication required — isolated execution environment
trust_level_requirements.T2_community: RECOMMENDED: apiKey or http bearer — lightweight access control
trust_level_requirements.T3_professional: REQUIRED: oauth2 — enterprise-grade authentication with scopes
trust_level_requirements.T4_enterprise: REQUIRED: oauth2 + RECOMMENDED: mutualTls — maximum security assurance

security_schemes.enforcement: Generator should auto-suggest appropriate security schemes based on trust_level. ThreeDimTest can verify scheme-trust alignment as advisory (not blocking for T1/T2). Programs published to remote registries (PL22) with T3+ SHOULD have security.authentication declared.
security_schemes.backward_compatibility: Programs without security.authentication remain valid. Trust_level (T1-T4) continues to govern internal permission boundaries independently.

identity_signing.description: Cryptographic signing specification for AIAP program identity. Provides publisher authenticity proof using JWS (JSON Web Signature), complementing governance_hash (SHA-256 integrity) with origin verification.

algorithm_support.primary: ES256 (ECDSA with P-256 curve and SHA-256) — recommended for broad compatibility

key_management.public_key_format: JWK Set (RFC 7517) hosted at publisher's well-known URL
key_management.key_rotation: Publishers SHOULD support key rotation via JWK Set kid (Key ID) field

signing_process: 1. Compute SHA-256 of AIAP.md content (excluding identity.signature field)
2. Create JWS payload with: { hash: sha256_hex, program_id: string, version: string, timestamp: ISO8601 }
3. Sign payload with publisher's private key using declared algorithm
4. Store JWS compact serialization in identity.signature.value

verification_integration.i11_check: I11 Program Identity Verification extended: if identity.signature present, verify JWS against public_key_url. PASS → SIGNATURE_VERIFIED. FAIL → SIGNATURE_INVALID (WARNING). Absent → SIGNATURE_NOT_PRESENT (INFO for local, WARNING for remote registry).
verification_integration.registry_requirement: Programs published to remote registries (PL22) with trust_level >= T3 MUST have identity.signature. Unsigned T3+ programs in remote registries flagged as REGISTRY_UNSIGNED WARNING.
verification_integration.package_integration: When packaging (.aiap archive), include identity.signature in manifest.json for end-to-end verification: package hash → governance_hash → identity.signature → publisher.

identity_signing.enforcement: Optional for local programs. Recommended for trust_level >= T3 with status='active'. Required for remote registry publication of T3+ programs. Absence is never a blocking error for local use.
identity_signing.backward_compatibility: Programs without identity.signature remain fully valid. All existing I11 checks continue to work — signature verification is additive.

content.note: This is a STANDARD extension file — not directly executable. Loaded by ThreeDimTest and ReadTemplate as supplementary quality checks.
